rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE DERRAH VS. GEICO INSURANCE CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2024, line 1. PETITIONER KATHERINE DERRAH'S Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award. Petitioner Katherine Derrah's petition to confirm arbitration award is GRANTED. If a petition to confirm is duly served and filed, "the court shall confirm the award as made," unless the award is corrected or vacated, or the proceeding is dismissed. (CCP 1286.) Here, all conditions have been met. The petition was "duly served" when Respondent Geico Insurance Company made a general appearance in the action by filing a "Response and Opposition" on November 15, 2024. (See CCP 410.50(a) ["[a] general appearance by a party is equivalent to personal service of summons on such party."].) Geico's reliance on Abers v. Rohrs (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 1199 is misplaced. There, the party contending there were defects in service of the petition had specially appeared via a motion to quash service. (Id. at 1205.) Geico does not set forth grounds sufficient to vacate or correct the award in its response. (CCP 1286.2, 1286.6.) In fact, Geico concedes, "the arbitrator correctly entered an award" and acknowledges it "did not contest the additional $38,432.45" in costs. (Response 5:13-27.) Confirmation is thus mandatory, and Geico cites no authority supporting its contention that the issue of confirmation is necessarily moot because Geico has issued two checks that it believes satisfies the award. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL PHILLIPS ET AL VS. LONDON N. BREED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Phillips Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And To Deem Admitted Requests For Admission
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Thursday, December 12, 2024, Line 4. DEFENDANTs LONDON BREED , IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS MAYOR OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JOSE CISNEROS, and CARMEN CHU's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Phillips Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And To Deem Admitted Requests For Admission. Defendants London Breed (in her official capacity as Mayor of the City and County of San Francisco), Jose Cisneros (in his official capacity as Treasurer of the City and County of San Francisco) and Carmen Chu's (in her official capacity as City Administrator of the City and County of San Francisco) Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Phillips Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And To Deem Admitted Requests For Admission, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Good cause appearing and the statutory criteria for such orders having been met, the court orders: (1) Plaintiff Michael Phillips must serve verified answers, without objection, to Form Interrogatories (Set Number One), served on Plaintiff via U.S. Mail on September 5, 2024, within twenty (20) days of service of notice of this order; (2) Plaintiff Michael Phillips must serve verified answers, without objection, to Special Interrogatories (Set Number One), served on Plaintiff via U.S. Mail on September 5, 2024, within twenty (20) days of service of notice of this order; (3) Plaintiff Michael Phillips must serve verified answers, without objection, to Request for Production of Documents (Set Number One), served on Plaintiff via U.S. Mail on September 5, 2024, within twenty (20) days of service of notice of this order; and, (4) Each request for admission to Plaintiff Michael Phillips made in the City's Request for Admission (Set Number One), served on Plaintiff via U.S. Mail on September 5, 2024, is deemed admitted. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CLARISSA A. MARQUEZ VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Thursday, December 12, 2024, Line 3. PLAINTIFF CLARISSA MARQUEZ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Plaintiff Clarissa A. Marquez's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Defendant American Honda Motor Company, Inc. is ordered to produce all documents responsive to Request #30 of Set One within 21 days of service of notice of this order. The request is not vague or unduly overbroad. Information regarding similar complaints, repairs, etc. is discoverable under the broad standard of CCP 2031.010. Whether Defendant has received similar complaints regarding similar cars or conducted-or been unable to conduct-repairs, for example, may well be relevant and admissible. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Co., supra, (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138, 143144, 153; Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 967, 973, 978-979, 986); thus, Plaintiff's requests for such information are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and Defendant's contentions and arguments do not establish otherwise. Defendant's refusal to produce any documents responsive to Request #30 is unreasonable. To the extent Plaintiff seeks additional relief, the motion is denied. Her request that all objections be overruled is unsupported by facts or law. Her request for further responses on other requests is unsupported by fact or law. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ).
